headline: From prime regularity exploration to generator-fingerprint research tooling.

change_dashboard.headline: PrimeProject moved from exploratory prime regularity visuals into publication-gated real-world generator fingerprint tooling.

maturity_ladder.signal: controlled attribution, null calibration, 8-setting replication audit, and scoped classifier baseline
OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go/Bitcoin collection targets, sample-power floors, handoff, submission contract, fixture audit, pre-intake lint, and intake validation
provenance, acceptance, promotion, handoff, submission-contract, lint-fixture, and intake gates before claims
5 falsification checks, 6 consistency checks, and controlled-synthetic-only claim floor

visual_rollup.headline: Visible change history: exploration scale, controlled signal, sim-to-real gates, and publication guardrails.

release_trail.title: Prime regularity demo
release_trail.proof: interactive gap, residue, and next-prime measure views

release_trail.title: Scale lift
release_trail.proof: larger local runs are visible on GitHub Pages without recomputation

release_trail.title: Controlled attribution
release_trail.proof: signal must survive bit-length control, null calibration, and replication

release_trail.title: Sim-to-real gates
release_trail.proof: OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go/Bitcoin baselines are registered but not accepted

release_trail.title: Publication guardrails
release_trail.proof: claim-language audit, claim ledger, lineage, decision protocol, falsification battery, and consistency audit prevent overclaiming

release_trail.title: Proof route pruning
release_trail.measure: 5/5 scalar clause-rank grammars refuted at 28 bits
release_trail.proof: TICKET-46 turns the Collatz scalar-rank route into a restricted no-go result, not a conjecture proof

release_trail.title: Stateful lasso pruning
release_trail.proof: TICKET-47 extracts a 16-edge positive-debt lasso and blocks zero-memory through last-4 edge-memory repairs

release_trail.title: Automaton reachability split
release_trail.proof: TICKET-48 blocks fixed finite total state repairs over the abstract lasso and isolates concrete reachability as the next theorem

evidence_flow.evidence: 10 task templates; 10 lint fixtures pass expectations; 0 submitted artifacts
public_demo_only; real-world and Bitcoin attribution blocked

hardening_map.risk: Submitted provenance can name the wrong baseline or library.
hardening_map.guard: provenance_record must match task baseline_id and library.

hardening_map.risk: A feature vector can be labelled as a different generator family.
hardening_map.guard: feature_vector_summary.label must match the task library.

hardening_map.risk: Top-level baseline/library fields can contradict the task.
hardening_map.guard: Optional record identity fields must match the task when present.

hardening_map.risk: A public artifact can leak local paths, URLs, or parent traversal.
hardening_map.guard: feature_vector_path must be a public relative path.

hardening_map.risk: Lint behavior can drift after contract changes.
hardening_map.guard: 10 public-safe fixtures replay pass/warn/block boundaries before intake.

evidence_spine.proof: The original browser experiment is now backed by larger reproducible local snapshots.

evidence_spine.gate: 48 grid rows, 5,000 null iterations, 8 replication settings
evidence_spine.proof: Generator-fingerprint claims are limited to controlled synthetic evidence that survives confound checks.

evidence_spine.gate: 10 targets, 10 public-safe fixtures, 0 accepted real submissions
evidence_spine.proof: OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go/Bitcoin collection is specified and lint-tested, but real aggregate baselines are not accepted yet.

evidence_spine.gate: 0 accepted baselines, 4 provenance rows blocked, 9,028 P0 samples projected
evidence_spine.proof: The project now states exactly why stronger real-world claims remain blocked.

evidence_spine.gate: 21 checked artifacts, 15 gates, 24 lineage nodes, 11 guard checks
evidence_spine.proof: Public statements are constrained by claim-language audit, checksums, lineage, decision rules, falsification guards, and consistency checks.

evidence_spine.gate: 5 scalar clause-rank grammars, 5 bounded suffix-memory repairs, and fixed finite total state repairs over the abstract lasso refuted; no full proof claim
evidence_spine.proof: The proof workbench now discards tested finite quotient repairs and requires symbolic reachability exclusion, a concrete periodic lift witness, or an ordinal infinite bridge.

latest_changes.label: Scale evidence
latest_changes.impact: The browser demo is now backed by 10M live compute plus static 1M/10M snapshots.

latest_changes.label: Controlled signal
latest_changes.impact: Synthetic generator fingerprints must survive bit-length controls, null calibration, and replication before they count.

latest_changes.label: Real-world gate
latest_changes.impact: OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go/Bitcoin targets are registered, but attribution remains blocked until accepted aggregate baselines arrive.

latest_changes.label: Submission discipline
latest_changes.impact: Collection contract, lint fixtures, and intake validation define exactly what public-safe evidence must contain.

latest_changes.label: Publication guardrail
latest_changes.impact: Claim-language audit, claim ledger, lineage, decision rules, falsification checks, and consistency checks keep the public page at demo/scaffold claim strength.

latest_changes.label: Proof route pruning
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-46 refutes all five tested Collatz scalar clause-rank grammars at 28 bits, so the remaining target is an ordinal/stateful infinite bridge.

latest_changes.label: Stateful lasso pruning
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-47 refutes bounded suffix-memory repairs on a 16-edge positive-debt pressure lasso; arbitrary automaton CEGIS and reachability remain open.

latest_changes.label: Automaton reachability split
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-48 refutes fixed finite total state repairs over the abstract lasso and shows the bounded concrete probe reaches only two positive steps, not a full lasso period.

research_delta.headline: What changed from the original prime-regularity demo to the current research scaffold.

claim_state.before: Exploratory prime regularity visualization with synthetic generator intuition.
claim_state.current: Controlled synthetic fingerprint evidence is null-calibrated and replication-audited, while real-world and Bitcoin attribution remain gated.
claim_state.next: Accepted OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go baselines plus labelled classifier vectors are required before real-world attribution claims can move.

tracks.current: 48-row attribution grid, 5,000 null iterations, 8-setting replication audit, 12 classifier vectors
5 registered baseline families, 10 collection targets, 10 handoff tasks, 10 submission templates, pre-intake lint, 10 fixture cases, 10 intake blockers, 0 accepted baselines
22 checksummed artifacts, claim-language audit, open-problem certificates, claim ledger, lineage DAG, decision protocol, falsification battery, consistency audit

tracks.before: Scalar rank and symbolic clause candidates could still look plausible on bounded horizons.
tracks.current: TICKET-46 refutes 5/5 tested finite template-local scalar clause-rank grammars; TICKET-47 refutes bounded suffix-memory repairs; TICKET-48 refutes fixed finite total state repairs over the abstract lasso and moves the target to reachability exclusion or a concrete periodic lift.

claim_lanes.basis: controlled grid + null + replication + scoped classifier
accepted baselines and classifier vectors missing
nonce-risk report and wallet baseline metadata missing

phases.label: Prime regularity plan
phases.summary: Defined the practical target as generator traces and anomaly detection, not raw prime prediction.

phases.label: Interactive Conjecture Lab
phases.summary: Built browser-side comparison of rejection, next_prime, and wheel observation measures up to 10M.

phases.label: 10M research snapshots
phases.summary: Bundled local precomputed SVG snapshots so GitHub Pages can show larger runs without recomputing.

phases.label: Bitcoin defensive track
phases.summary: Separated public secp256k1 constants from practical nonce and signature metadata risk.

phases.label: Generator fingerprint baseline
phases.summary: Added residue, low-bit, and local prime-gap fingerprints with baseline distance comparison.

phases.label: Controlled attribution grid
phases.summary: Added paired uncontrolled and bit-length-controlled experiments with repeat confidence intervals.

phases.label: Null calibration
phases.summary: Calibrates controlled attribution profiles against a row-structured random-label null with family-wise profile selection control.

phases.label: Replication audit
phases.summary: Audits whether null-calibrated controlled profiles replicate across limit, train-count, and test-count settings.

phases.label: Crypto-classifier baseline
phases.summary: Exports fixed-length controlled synthetic feature vectors and runs a nearest-centroid classifier while keeping real-world attribution gated.

phases.label: Real-world baseline registry
phases.summary: Registered OpenSSL, BoringSSL, Go, Bitcoin Core, and wallet/library families while keeping sensitive raw data local.

phases.label: Real-world collection matrix
phases.summary: Defines matched 2048/3072/4096-bit RSA and Bitcoin signature metadata targets before claims can advance.

phases.label: Sample power calibration
phases.summary: Estimates conservative TV-drift screening floors so sample counts are not treated as stronger evidence than they support.

phases.label: Provenance requirements
phases.summary: Defines required metadata and forbidden public fields before real-world samples can support attribution claims.

phases.label: Provenance audit
phases.summary: Audits filled provenance records for missing metadata, checksum format, and forbidden public sensitive fields before attribution claims.

phases.label: Baseline acceptance
phases.summary: Combines collection availability, provenance audit, and sample-power tier before a real-world baseline can be used for attribution claims.

phases.label: Promotion plan
phases.summary: Identifies the minimal OpenSSL/BoringSSL-style unlock path and required sample floor before accepted real-world evidence is possible.

phases.label: Collection handoff
phases.summary: Converts baseline gates into prioritized public-safe collection tasks with provenance, sample floor, and classifier-scope constraints.

phases.label: Submission contract
phases.summary: Exports machine-readable public submission templates that bind task IDs, optional record identity, public feature-vector paths, sample floors, checksums, provenance identity, feature vectors, and forbidden private fields.

phases.label: Submission lint
phases.summary: Runs pre-intake checks over candidate public submission records so collectors can fix contract, checksum, feature-vector, duplicate, and public-safety errors before claim gates.

phases.label: Fixture audit
phases.summary: Runs public-safe pass, warning, missing-feature, forbidden-field, provenance-identity, feature-label, record-identity, feature-path, and reused-checksum fixtures against the submission linter before real collector data is accepted.

phases.label: Collection intake
phases.summary: Validates submitted aggregate artifacts against handoff tasks before they can become accepted real-world baselines.

phases.label: Research readiness scoring
phases.summary: Scores Sim-to-Real, attribution validation, classifier data, and Bitcoin integration before allowing stronger claims.

phases.label: Evidence pack gates
phases.summary: Bundles artifact checksums, publication gates, claim level, and local collection protocols.

phases.label: Claim-language audit
phases.summary: Scans README, docs, and GitHub Pages copy so public wording cannot exceed the current evidence boundary.

phases.label: Claim ledger
phases.summary: Maps each public-facing claim to the gates and artifacts that allow, qualify, or block it.

phases.label: Artifact lineage
phases.summary: Builds an acyclic dependency graph over public artifacts and checks evidence-pack checksums against current files.

phases.label: Decision protocol
phases.summary: Applies pre-registered publication and claim-promotion rules to evidence, claim, and lineage reports.

phases.label: Falsification battery
phases.summary: Runs negative-control, bit-length confound, and claim-promotion checks before attribution claims can be upgraded.

phases.label: Publication consistency
phases.summary: Checks that evidence, claim, decision, and falsification artifacts agree on the same high-risk claim boundary.

open_gaps.gap: Need at least two available aggregate generator baselines before real attribution claims.
Need labelled feature vectors across OpenSSL, BoringSSL, Go, and suspicious samples.
Need a bundled Bitcoin nonce-risk report from owned or public metadata summaries.
